opportunities.company_name: ExxonMobil vs Utilities Select Sector ETF
opportunities.rationale: XOM at $162.62 (May 19) — energy sector outperforming as oil holds above $70 WTI. XLU at $44.34, utilities under pressure as 10Y yields remain elevated. Spread trade thesis intact: energy FCF generation vs rate-sensitive utilities de-rating. XOM 20d MA ~$155, 50d MA ~$148; strong uptrend. XLU 20d MA ~$44, near resistance. Invalidated if WTI drops below $65 or rates rally sharply dovish supporting XLU.
opportunities.trade_setup: sector rotation: energy long / utilities short
opportunities.entry_trigger: Enter XOM long on pullback to $160-$162 zone (above 20d MA); short XLU at $44.00-$44.50 on any bounce toward resistance.

opportunities.rationale: CVX at $197.37, OXY at $60.70 (May 19). Within energy sector: CVX is the quality/defensive play with 4%+ dividend yield and downstream cushion; OXY is the highest-beta pure upstream name (Permian-heavy, leveraged balance sheet). CVX 20d MA ~$189, 50d MA ~$183 — in uptrend. OXY 20d MA ~$58, 50d MA ~$55 — OXY extended above both MAs, short pressure building. In bullish regime with moderate risk appetite, quality outperforms high-beta within sector. Invalidated if WTI spikes above $90 (strongly favouring OXY).
opportunities.entry_trigger: Long CVX at $195-$197 (pullback to 20d MA zone); short OXY at $60.50-$61.50 on any intraday extension.

opportunities.company_name: Technology Select Sector ETF vs Industrials Select Sector ETF
opportunities.rationale: XLK at $173.24, XLI at $168.74 (May 19). XLK pulled back from highs — AI earnings cycle (NVDA beat confirmed) provides support. XLI at $168.74 — industrials elevated, vulnerable to tariff uncertainty and ISM softness. XLK 20d MA ~$168, 50d MA ~$162; above both MAs. XLI 20d MA ~$170, now below 20d MA indicating relative weakness building. NVDA catalyst confirmed as XLK long support. Invalidated if Flash PMI May strong beat drives XLI outperformance or XLK breaks below $163.
opportunities.trade_setup: tech AI earnings cycle vs industrial laggard
opportunities.entry_trigger: Enter XLK long at $171-$173 (support from 20d MA zone); short XLI at $169-$171 (below broken 20d MA support).

opportunities.company_name: Costco Wholesale vs SPDR S&P Retail ETF
opportunities.rationale: COST at $1,094.32, XRT at $79.13 (May 19). Consumer Defensive vs Discretionary rotation: COST is the defensive leader (warehouse model, membership fees provide earnings floor) while XRT broad discretionary retail is exposed to tariff headwinds and consumer sentiment softness. COST 20d MA ~$1,055, 50d MA ~$1,020 — strong above both. XRT at $79.13 — near 20d MA resistance; after-hours spike to $81.23 shows volatility, not confirmed breakout. WMT earnings May 21 catalyst for defensive consumer. Invalidated if consumer discretionary strongly beats and XRT sustains above $84.
opportunities.entry_trigger: Long COST at $1,080-$1,090 on any intraday dip (above 20d MA); short XRT at $79-$80 zone (resistance confluence).

opportunities.company_name: EOG Resources vs Energy Select Sector ETF
opportunities.rationale: EOG at $144.30, XLE at $61.29 (May 19). EOG is the highest-quality pure E&P name within energy sector: low breakevens (~$40 WTI), capital discipline, strong FCF. XLE ETF includes integrated majors with downstream compression diluting upside. EOG 20d MA ~$138, 50d MA ~$132 — well above both. XLE 20d MA ~$59, 50d MA ~$57 — both MAs below current price. LOW conviction — intra-sector pair requires z-score >1.5 confirmation from live charting. Invalidated if WTI drops below $65 (narrows EOG premium) or XLE rallies on integrated major outperformance.
opportunities.trade_setup: intra-energy quality E&P vs broad ETF
opportunities.entry_trigger: Enter only if EOG/XLE ratio confirms widening spread; long EOG at $142-$144, short XLE at $61-$62. Requires manual chart verification pre-entry.
